topic.what: LLM-generated obfuscated XSS payloads evaluated for runtime behavior preservation
topic.how: Pipeline combining deterministic transformation chains, behavior-filtered fine-tuning, and browser-based runtime validation to compare observable execution behavior
topic.why_matters: Assesses whether LLM-generated obfuscations preserve functional behavior for downstream ML-based XSS detection; determines if generated samples can improve training data diversity without introducing noise

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of LLM-generated payloads that reproduce same browser-observed behavior as original payload
characteristics.evidence: Table II: 100 generated payloads per model evaluated via browser-based runtime comparison
characteristics.context: Fine-tuned on 88 behavior-filtered source-target pairs; baseline is untuned model

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of deterministic obfuscation chains that preserve runtime behavior under browser execution
characteristics.evidence: Table I: 200 chains evaluated from 1000 generated; 88 behavior-filtered vs 112 non-matching
characteristics.context: Chain-level validation; each chain applies two-step transformation recipe to original malicious payload

characteristics.what_itasures: Validity rate of different transformation pair combinations
characteristics.evidence: Table III: split_strings→comment_insertion achieves 0.769; hex_escape→split_strings achieves 0.028
characteristics.context: String manipulation pairs more stable; encoding-heavy pairs introduce execution failures

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classification accuracy of Random Forest detector under different training conditions
characteristics.evidence: Table IV: 80/20 stratified split; TF-IDF character n-grams (3-5); 100 estimators
characteristics.context: Generated samples form small fraction of full dataset; dominated by original distribution

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score of downstream XSS detector
characteristics.evidence: Table IV: all conditions achieve F1 > 0.997
characteristics.context: 3 conditions tested: original only, original + all generated, original + behavior-filtered only

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of labeled XSS payloads in dataset
characteristics.evidence: Table I: malicious samples used as source for obfuscation generation
characteristics.context: Malicious portion serves as source for obfuscation chains; benign portion used in downstream classification

good_for: evaluating LLM-generated obfuscation quality for web security
behavior-based validation of generated security examples
not_for: improving detector performance via augmentation in current setting
generalizing to complex multi-step obfuscation beyond 2-step recipes
requires: controlled browser environment for runtime evaluation
behavior-filtered training pairs for effective fine-tuning
access to labeled benign/malicious XSS dataset

novelty: Browser-based runtime evaluation procedure for XSS payload validation instead of syntactic similarity
Behavior-filtered fine-tuning using validated source-target obfuscation pairs
Pipeline combining deterministic transformation chains with LLM generation for XSS obfuscation
Empirical comparison of baseline vs fine-tuned LLM on behavior preservation (46.7% relative improvement)
open_problems: Low absolute match rate (22%) even after fine-tuning; most generated payloads fail to preserve behavior
Runtime checker limited to controlled environment; may not capture all browser/application context effects
Fine-tuning set is relatively small (88 pairs); limits generalization to complex obfuscation strategies
Neural classifier evaluation not performed; uncertain effect on models beyond RF + TF-IDF
Constrained decoding and transformation-aware generation not explored
